gaps: No test covers DFU failure at >90% completion with subsequent firmware version reversion after device restart. Existing tests (66363556, 66363557, 66363558) only cover successful DFU completion scenarios, not partial failure at high percentage.

No test covers language change failure at >90% completion with language reversion after device restart. Test 66363558 covers language not persisting on charge case but under different conditions (earbuds on outdated firmware, case already up to date), not a >90% failure scenario.

No test validates firmware version display accuracy after a failed DFU + device power cycle. The bug scenario specifically checks if the version shown as 'up-to-date' after failure actually reverts on restart.
No test covers the intermittent nature of this failure mode. All existing tests assume deterministic success or failure, not flaky behavior that requires multiple repetitions to reproduce.

No test validates the state of the device during the critical >90% window — e.g., what happens if the device is briefly used, battery dips, or Bluetooth disconnects during the final 10% of a DFU or language update.

No test covers the interaction between DFU failure and language change failure as related symptoms of the same root cause (partial completion corruption). The bug treats them as parallel failure modes.
No lifecycle testing around DFU failure: what happens to the app UI state (progress bar, status messages, error handling) when the app is backgrounded/foregrounded during a >90% failure scenario.

No test validates that after a failed DFU at >90%, the device is left in a consistent state (e.g., partially updated components, mismatched firmware between left/right earbud and charge case on Voyager Free 60+).

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-001: DFU Failure at >90% — Firmware Version Reversion After Device Restart (Voyager Free 60+ UC)
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record current firmware version (e.g., v1.2.3).
3. Trigger a firmware update via the DFU notification in the app.
4. Monitor the progress bar on the DFU screen. Allow the update to proceed past 90% completion.
5. At >90% (e.g., 93-97%), simulate a failure condition: either force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app OR disconnect Bluetooth from the phone's settings.
6. Wait 30 seconds, then reconnect Bluetooth and reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Observe the DFU completion screen or error notification.
8. Navigate to Device → Device Info and read the displayed firmware version.
9. Power cycle the Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds (place in charge case, close lid for 10s, reopen, remove earbuds).
10. Reconnect to the app and navigate to Device → Device Info again.
11. Compare the firmware version displayed before the DFU, after the DFU failure, and after the power cycle.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version displayed after the power cycle reverts to the pre-DFU version (e.g., v1.2.3), confirming the partial update did not commit. The app may show an error or 'Update Failed' message, but the version should not show the new version as 'installed'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-002: DFU Failure at >90% — Firmware Version Displayed as 'Up-to-Date' but Reverts on Restart
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record current firmware version (e.g., v1.2.3).
3. Trigger a firmware update via the DFU notification.
4. Allow the update to reach >90% completion (e.g., 95%).
5. At >90%, simulate a Bluetooth disconnection by turning off Bluetooth on the phone.
6. Wait 15 seconds, then re-enable Bluetooth and allow the device to reconnect.
7. Open Poly Studio Mobile app. Observe if the app shows a success screen or 'Firmware up to date' message.
8. Navigate to Device → Device Info and note the firmware version displayed.
9. Without power cycling, check if the version shown is the new version (e.g., v1.3.0) or the old version (v1.2.3).
10. Power cycle the device: place earbuds in charge case, close lid for 30s, reopen, remove earbuds.
11. Reconnect to the app and navigate to Device → Device Info again.
12. Compare the firmware version before DFU, after reconnection (step 8), and after power cycle (step 11).
generated_testcases.expected: If the app incorrectly shows the new firmware version as 'up-to-date' after reconnection (step 7-8), the version MUST revert to the pre-DFU version after the power cycle (step 11-12). This validates the bug scenario where the version display is misleading until a restart exposes the true state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-003: Language Change Failure at >90% — Language Reversion After Device Restart
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language.
3. Record the current language (e.g., English).
4. Select a different language (e.g., French) and begin the language update process.
5. Monitor the progress bar on the charge case touchscreen (if available) or app. Allow the update to proceed past 90% completion.
6. At >90% (e.g., 94%), simulate a failure: either force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app OR briefly remove one earbud from the charge case and reinsert it.
7. Wait 20 seconds, then reopen the app and reconnect to the device.
8. Observe the language displayed on the charge case touchscreen (if available) and in the app's Language setting.
9. Power cycle the device: place both earbuds in the charge case, close lid for 30s, reopen.
10. Remove earbuds, reconnect to the app, and navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language.
11. Use the device for 2-3 minutes (play media, make a short call), then check the Language setting again.
12. Compare the language setting at each step.
generated_testcases.expected: After the failure at >90%, the language may temporarily appear changed (e.g., French on the charge case screen), but after the power cycle and brief use, the language reverts to the original language (English). This confirms the partial language update did not commit.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-004: Language Change Failure at >90% — Charge Case vs. Earbud Language Mismatch After Partial Update
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language. Set language to English.
3. Initiate a language change to German.
4. Allow the update to reach >90% completion on the progress indicator.
5. At >90%, simulate a failure by disconnecting Bluetooth from the phone's quick settings panel.
6. Wait 10 seconds, then reconnect Bluetooth.
7. Open the app and navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language.
8. Observe the language setting displayed in the app.
9. Check the charge case touchscreen display language (if available).
10. Remove the left earbud and check any voice prompts language.
11. Remove the right earbud and check any voice prompts language.
12. Power cycle the device (close lid 30s, reopen).
13. Reconnect and check all three: app language setting, charge case display language, and earbud voice prompt language.
generated_testcases.expected: After the partial failure, there may be a mismatch between the charge case display language and earbud voice prompt language (e.g., charge case shows German, earbuds still speak English). After power cycle, all components should revert to the original language (English), confirming the partial update did not fully commit across all device components.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-005: Intermittent Failure Mode — Repeated DFU Attempts at >90% to Reproduce Flaky Behavior
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record current firmware version.
3. Trigger a firmware update.
4. Allow the update to reach >90% completion.
5. At >90%, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Reopen the app, reconnect, and observe the result.
7. Repeat steps 2-6 for a total of 5 iterations, each time recording:
a. The exact percentage at which the app was killed.
b. Whether the firmware version changed or reverted after restart.
c. Any error messages or UI state displayed.
8. After the 5th iteration, perform a full device power cycle (close lid 30s, reopen).
9. Navigate to Device → Device Info and confirm the final firmware version.
10. Document the number of times the version appeared to update vs. reverted.
generated_testcases.expected: The failure is intermittent — not all 5 iterations may exhibit the reversion bug. At least 1-2 iterations should show the firmware version reverting after restart, demonstrating the flaky nature of the >90% failure mode. The final version after all iterations should match the original pre-DFU version.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-006: Device State During Critical >90% Window — Battery Dip During Final 10% of DFU
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Ensure the charge case battery is between 20-30% (low but not critical).
3. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record current firmware version.
4. Trigger a firmware update.
5. Allow the update to reach >90% completion.
6. At >90%, place the charge case on a wireless charger to simulate battery fluctuation during charging.
7. Alternatively, if battery is low enough, allow the charge case battery to dip below 10% during the update.
8. Observe the DFU progress bar behavior — does it stall, jump, or show an error?
9. After the update completes or fails, navigate to Device → Device Info.
10. Power cycle the device (close lid 30s, reopen).
11. Reconnect and check the firmware version again.
12. Check the battery level display for the charge case and both earbuds.
generated_testcases.expected: A battery dip during the critical >90% window may cause the DFU to fail silently. The firmware version should revert to the pre-DFU version after power cycle. The battery level display should remain accurate. The device should not enter an unrecoverable state (bricked) — it should remain functional for calls and media.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-007: App UI State During >90% DFU Failure — Background/Foreground Lifecycle
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Trigger a firmware update.
3. Allow the update to reach >90% completion.
4. At >90%, press the Home button to background the app.
5. Wait 30 seconds, then reopen the app from the recent apps list.
6. Observe the DFU progress bar — does it resume, reset, or show an error?
7. Note any status messages displayed.
8. Background the app again (Home button), wait 10 seconds, then force-kill the app from the app switcher.
9. Reopen the app and reconnect to the device.
10. Observe the UI state: is there a DFU completion screen, an error dialog, or a prompt to retry?
11. Navigate to Device → Device Info and check the firmware version.
12. Power cycle the device and check the firmware version again.
generated_testcases.expected: After backgrounding and foregrounding, the progress bar may resume or show an error. After force-kill and reopen, the app should handle the failed state gracefully — showing an appropriate error message or retry prompt, not a false success. The firmware version should revert after power cycle.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-008: Combined DFU + Language Failure — Parallel Symptom Validation
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record firmware version (e.g., v1.2.3).
3. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and record current language (e.g., English).
4. Trigger a firmware update and allow it to reach >90% completion.
5. At >90%, simulate failure by disconnecting Bluetooth.
6. Reconnect and observe the DFU result.
7. Without power cycling, immediately navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and initiate a language change to Spanish.
8. Allow the language update to reach >90% completion.
9. At >90%, simulate failure by force-killing the app.
10. Reopen the app, reconnect, and navigate to Device → Device Info and Device → Device Settings → Language.
11. Power cycle the device (close lid 30s, reopen).
12. Reconnect and check both firmware version and language setting.
13. Use the device for 3-5 minutes (play music, make a call), then recheck both settings.
generated_testcases.expected: Both the DFU and language change should exhibit the same failure pattern — partial completion at >90% followed by reversion after power cycle. The firmware version should revert to v1.2.3 and the language should revert to English after the power cycle and brief use. This confirms both symptoms share the same root cause (partial completion corruption).

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-009: Component State Consistency After >90% DFU Failure — Left/Right Earbud and Charge Case Firmware Mismatch
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record the firmware versions for: left earbud, right earbud, and charge case (if displayed separately).
3. Trigger a firmware update.
4. Allow the update to reach >90% completion.
5. At >90%, remove the left earbud from the charge case while keeping the right earbud in the case.
6. Wait 10 seconds, then reinsert the left earbud.
7. Observe the DFU progress bar behavior.
8. After the update completes or fails, navigate to Device → Device Info.
9. Check the firmware version for each component: left earbud, right earbud, charge case.
10. Power cycle the device (close lid 30s, reopen).
11. Reconnect and check all three component firmware versions again.
12. Test basic functionality: play audio through left earbud, right earbud, and verify charge case touchscreen responsiveness.
generated_testcases.expected: After the >90% failure with one earbud removed, there may be a firmware version mismatch between the left and right earbuds (e.g., left on old version, right on partially updated version). After power cycle, all components should revert to the original pre-DFU firmware version. The device should remain functional — no component should be bricked or unresponsive.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-010: DFU Failure at >90% — Bluetooth Disconnect During Final 10% with Auto-Reconnect
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Free 60+ UC.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Info and record current firmware version.
3. Trigger a firmware update.
4. Allow the update to reach >90% completion (e.g., 92%).
5. At >90%, turn off Bluetooth on the phone for exactly 5 seconds, then turn it back on.
6. Allow the device to auto-reconnect to the phone.
7. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and observe the UI state.
8. Note any error messages, retry prompts, or success indicators.
9. Navigate to Device → Device Info and check the firmware version.
10. Without power cycling, check if the device functions normally (play media, adjust volume via charge case touchscreen).
11. Power cycle the device (close lid 30s, reopen).
12. Reconnect and check the firmware version again.
13. Verify all audio settings (ANC, Transparency, EQ) are preserved and functional.
generated_testcases.expected: After the brief Bluetooth disconnect at >90%, the DFU should fail. The app should display an appropriate error (not a false success). The firmware version should revert after power cycle. All audio settings should remain at their pre-DFU values. The device should remain fully functional for calls, media, and charge case touchscreen operations.
